title: VISIT SEOUL NET Brand Film
summary: 'All About Traveling Seoul,' a branding video for the Seoul Tourism Organization's VSN(VisitSeoul.net).
overview: The Grap captured the first step of traveling, and every moment of Seoul, a city where the past, present, and future coexist. By connecting the spellings of VISIT with Seoul's landmarks, it expresses a simple and intuitive feeling.
credits: Creative Director: Jungkwon Kim / Project Manager & Motion Design: Jongseok Lee / 2D Illustration: Mijin Seo

title: Wellune App
summary: Wellune is a sleep-tech service from BitSensing, an image processing radar technology startup.
overview: Wellune proposes a way for users to get a comfortable and healthy sleep by analyzing sleep data such as sleep patterns and sleep environments by linked sensors. Midnight Blue, reminiscent of the night sky, was applied as the main color to create an atmosphere related to sleep, and we used micro-interaction properly to make users have more interesting and fun experiences when using the app. Also, each situation setting and status is provided with friendly illustration and animation guide to provide a design that is easy for users of various ages to understand.
credits: Creative Director by Kim Hanil, Seo Mijin / Project Manager by Kim Hanil / UI Design by Kim Hanil, Seo Mijin, Choi Sunhyeong / Illustration Design by Lim Hyejin / Motion Design by Lee Jongseok

title: Wellune for business
summary: Wellune is a sleep-tech service from BitSensing, an image processing radar technology startup.
overview: Wellune proposes a way for users to get a comfortable and healthy sleep by analyzing sleep data such as sleep patterns and sleep environments by linked sensors. The Grap successfully renewed the app service design of Wellune and added a B2B version of Wellune for Business based on a high understanding of the service. Consistent and state-aware UI and graphic images were applied to enable a large number of unspecified sleep managers to monitor the manager screen efficiently, maintain the identity of the app, and implement a simple but trendy design.
credits: Creative Director by Kim Hanil / Project Manager by Kim Hanil / UI Design by Kim Hanil, Choi Sunhyung, Park Soohyun

title: LG Sustainable Village QR Promotion Mobile Web
summary: This is a mobile environment optimized webpage work for the IFA 2023 LG Electronics Showroom event in Berlin, Germany.
overview: Event participants can experience various areas in LG Electronics' exhibition hall through the webpage and accomplish challenges by scanning the QR code of each area. We wanted to actively use LG Electronics' brand image and elements to give connectivity with the exhibition hall. Also, interactive elements and smooth flows were considered due to the circumstances of participants' movement around the exhibition hall.
credits: Project Manager by Shin Geonwoong, Creative director by Shin Geonwoong, UI Design by Shin Geonwoong, Park Soohyun, Web Publishing by Seo Jonghoon, Development by Bae Minyoung

title: SKON 2.0 App
summary: SKON is a mobile company life convenience service that can be used by all members of the company together.
overview: By utilizing the functions necessary for work, such as attendance management, facility reservation, and digital authorization, users can not only handle work efficiently regardless of time and place, but also communicate with various interactions such as birthday celebration or employment day celebration and bulletin boards that can be enjoyed as small groups for positive group culture. Also, it has increased the convenience of company members by providing various functions such as cafe smart order located in the building, checking the menu and congestion of the cafeteria.
credits: Project Manager by Park Byung-chune Creative director by Kim Hanil Design UX by Park Byung-chune, Hak Jangki Design UI by Kim Hanil, Lim Hyejin

title: Better Micro Website
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Motion Graphic, Web Publishing, Development
summary: Better is a recording service that helps you make yourself better through your daily records.
overview: Optimized for light records, Better shows the process of becoming a better me with accumulated records. We created a micro-website using the brand's tone and manner to make more users aware of this service.
credits: Project Manager by Shin Geonwoong / Creative director by Shin Geonwoong / UI Design by Shin Geonwoong, Kim Jeesung / Motion Design by Gil Youngjin, Shin Geonwoong, Kim Jeesung

title: Ophily Brand Website
summary: Opilly is a brand that delivers colorful and delicious happiness through food essential to customers' livestyle.
overview: We have organized this website with a clean and reliable design to capture the confidence contained in Ophilly's food and authenticity toward customers. Designs with consistent brand tone and margins to highlight the crucial information helps users to explore and buy products with recognizing the brand's identity and having satisfactory consumption.
credits: Creative Director by Shin Geonwoong / Project Manager by Shin Geonwoong / Design UI by Shin Geonwoong, Kim Jeesung, Lim Hyejin / Web Publishing by Designart / Back-end & Admin Development by Designart

title: HYPHEN PAY APP
summary: Hyphenpay is a mobile payment service for the MZ generation.
overview: By using Hyphenpay, you can easily pay without cash or card in the laundry room of every partner university dormitories. It is designed to leave only the functions that users mainly use on the home screen so that even the first users can understand and use the service quickly. It supports reasonable and useful services for users by adding various functions as well as receiving benefits. The purple & white color combination provides strong visual contrast and allows expression according to the importance of the content. The Grap also applied motion graphic that a circle is being combined with a line after being saperated to imply the message of the slogan '#EasyPayWithoutCash #WithoutCard,' which includes the goal of Hyphenpay's service. It is a graphic to help users understand, which enhances the delivery of meaning and contains all the trendy charms.
credits: Project Manager by Kim Yeonghak / Design UX by Kim Yeonghak / Design UI by Kang Taewoo, Seo Mijin, Choi Sunhyung / Interaction Design by Lee Jongseok / Programming by Park Seongjun, Seo Gyuhyeon, Lee Seokjun, Kwon Yongsik

title: KIA K5 Beijing Motor Show
summary: Dynamic Driving
overview: The Grap designed the opening video of K5's Beijing Motor Show Countdown under the concept of "Dynamic Running." Starting with a beauty cut of K5's soft exterior, the cool and dynamic driving was expressed in the foreground of the virtual city. The countdown number of the opening was made of K5's key color, red, as points, and consists of a huge sculpture to create a situation where you run and avoid obstacles, allowing audiences to feel a sense of urgency and speed.
credits: Creative Director: Ahn Changhyeon, Lee Jongseok / 3D Modeling: Kim Sanghee / 3D Animation: Kim Sanghee, Lee Jongseok / 2D Animation: Lee Jongseok / Composition: Lee Jongseok Editor: Lee Jongseok

title: Samhwa Paint Website
summary: Samhwa Paint viewed paint as a concept that protects life and protects the environment, rather than just coloring materials, and reorganized its website to immerse itself deeper into customers' spaces and lives.
overview: Those who visit the Samhwa Paint website range from beginners to experts. The biggest goal was to carefully select the right information for different consumer groups and to create an environment where they could easily and quickly access the information they wanted. The website became worthy to everyone by focusing on the natural flow of the contents so that customers can get the idea for the color and the information about the offline stores where they can buy the products. The second goal of improvement, which is to enhance the brand image and share corporate value, has made significant changes to the visuals. The main banner size catches the eye with a large typography on a full-size layout and creates a differentiated visual by harmoniously arranging serif and san serif fonts. Background visuals support image or video formats according to the purpose of operation, allowing you to choose according to the situation, creating an environment for operational efficiency and broad design. Third, Samhwa Paint wanted to provide customers with expertise and pride in colors through special experiences. The Grap selected clean and clear images and designed concisely without any elements that hinder immersion, satisfying both aesthetics and usability. In color search, it accumulates calmly every time you select a color you searched for, and it is being completed in a shape close to a real paper color chip, resulting in speciality and empathy. Color is being boldly shown on a full screen using a web environment, so that customers could focus more on the essence of color.
credits: ( The Grap ) Chief Advisor_ Kim Jungkwon / Creative Director_ Seo Mijin / Project Manager_ Kim Taewoo, Seo Mijin / UX Design_ Kim Taewoo / UI Design_ Seo Mijin / Web Publishing_ Jeong Euiyoung / Programming: Kang Mihyeon, Park Minsu, Min Changhun ( Samhwa Paint ) Programming: Seo Yeongbin

title: OnDream Society Website
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Web Publishing, Development
summary: The OnDream Society is a branded community space created by Hyundai Motor's Chung Mong-koo Foundation.
overview: It is a community-based platform that creatively and innovatively solves social problems related to the environment with future generations, and is the first space platform of the Chung Mong-koo Foundation. The Grap created the website thinking about how to convey the philosophy and story of the space with an impactful and consistent voice on the website. The green atmosphere and the blue points of the space are presented in a fresh and harmonious manner within the shape of Circle & House. The main message of the brand is written in big typo, which has a simple yet intense delivery power. Focusing on the meaning of 'together' used repeatedly in the brand slogans 'THINK TOGETHER, LEARN TOGETHER, CREATE TOGETHER', we added calm interactions that can be truly remembered to the visiters.
credits: ( Hyundai Motor Chung Mong-Koo Foundation ) Project Manager by Shin jinam, Jang Hyejin ( The Grap ) Chief Advisor by Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager by Go Yeounk Design UX by Go Yeonuk / Design UI by Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin, Lim Hyejin / Interaction Design by Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong, Lee Jongseok, Yeon Jinil / Web Publishing by Yeon Jinil / Back-end & Admin Development by Bae Minyoung ( Storidaa ) Website Branding by Cho Yunjeong

title: Episode Website
summary: The Grap have reorganized the website of Episode, a residential cultural platform for a better urban life.
overview: We have improved the main page and reservation page so that users can conveniently access and experience the news and visit reservations, while maintaining the feeling of collage illustrations on existing sites that express different personalities and lifestyles. Alsp, we have organized Instagram real-time linkages so that users can immediately access and participate in latest news from Episode. We have built a new We do category to share the story that the Episode wants to tell, provide various events, and enjoy a variety of different residential lives.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Hanil / Creative Director: Kim Hanil / UI Design: Kim Hanil, Lim Hyejin, Choi Sunhyung, Kim Seula / Web Publishing: Yeon Jinil, Seo Jonghoon / Development: Bae Minyoung

title: Episode App 2.0
whatWeDid: UX Plan, UI Design, Web Publishing
summary: Make your life more edgy!
overview: The Episode app is a platform where you can easily use everything from Episode, the largest single shared residential brand in Korea. In order to maintain the visual brand identity of Episode, we used existing collage illustrations and icon styles, and modularized them into card-type UI to quickly expand and reflect services provided differently for each branch. Various curation services are provided by sharing the lifestyle and cultural experiences of people with different personalities through community bulletin boards in the form of SNS and synchronizing the accumulated information from application for visiting tours to the time of completion of the contract. IoT linkage allows users to control various home appliances, and even requests for non-face-to-face reservations and repairs tailored to the untact era! We considered minimizing the inconvenience that tenants may feel while living and to deliver a message about the nature of the residence pursued by Episode. I hope you will experience a different residential life like never before, as well as work-life balance through Episode App 2.0.
credits: Project Manager by Kim Hanil / Creative director by Kim Hanil Design / UX by Lee Eunsil, Kim Eunho, Kwak Minjae / Design UI by Kim Hanil, Kim Seula, Seo Mijin

title: Coway Sleep Care Jam
summary: What is "good sleep"?
overview: Experience "special sleep" in a sleep-focused space. Coway Sleep Care 'Zam' is Coway's first mattress experience store, and it is a special space prepared for customers of Coway, from total home care solutions to mattress experiences that fit me to massage chair experiences. For the customer's sleep environment and cozy daily life, we set "Natural" & "Minimal" and "Modern" as design keywords that blends into the interior of the entire experience zone, including kiosks and mobile, to conduct UX and UI work and kiosk installation across the experience zone.
credits: Creative Director: Seo Mijin / UX Design: JS / UI Design: Kim Jeesung / Web Publishing: Seo Jonghoon, Seo Nayeon / Programing & Installing a kiosk: TOV

title: LGE ThinQ Home App
summary: Smart comfort, built right into your everyday.
overview: Together with LGE, The Grap has created a Home IoT application that will be serviced simultaneously in a new apartment complex. We made it simple but convenient for new household members to use so that they could be more satisfied after moving in. In addition to lighting and heating in the house, users can easily connect and control home appliances no matter if is built-in, new, or existing one so that users can easily control them whenever, and where-ever.
credits: Chief Advisor by Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager by Shin Geonwoong / Design UX by Kwak Minjae, Park Hyegyeong, Lee Eunsil, Kim Huiran / Design UI by Shin Geonwoong, Bae Youjin, Seo Mijin

title: Hanhwa Life ONSURE
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Web Publishing
summary: From recommending essential insurance to easy subscriptions for reasonable prices.
overview: Hanwha Life Insurance's Onsure Direct Insurance is a service of Hanwha Life to make it easier and faster to process complex insurance subscriptions by suggesting necessary insurance to customers through customized product recommendations and skipping unnecessary procedures of the subscription process. It is designed to maintain Hanwha Life's brand image, tone and manner by utilizing icons, illustration styles, and colors in each insurance product and service screen in unified sense.
credits: Project Manager: Shin Geonwoong / UX Design: Lee Eunsil / UI Design: Bae Youjin, Kim Seula, Lim Hyejin / Web Publishing: Lim Myeongseon

title: ELLE shop
summary: Taste Discovery Select Shop. Shop My Taste
overview: Elle Shop is a personal shopping service provided by Elle, a fashion magazine that discovers tastes in styling content created by professional style creators and quickly connects them to brand malls. It can be distinguished from the existing Elle Main and has adopted a simple social structure as a way to capture the core elements of Elle Shop. In familiar content, users can clip and take out useful styles of their taste at any time, and quickly access to new products and trends provided by editors.
credits: Project Manager by Kim Hanil, Lee Eunsil / UX Design: Lee Eunsil, Kim Soi / UI Design: Kim Hanil, Seo Mijin / Programing: Yoon Sungyu, JTBC PLUS

title: Around US Website
summary: Around US, always by our side
overview: The Around US website contains the wish of an artist who wants to reach out to many people who have waited with new content tomorrow more often than today, and better song today than yesterday. In the main page, news from various channels such as YouTube and Twitter were provided at a glance. In order to express the minimal sensibility and information delivery of Around US as a priority, the graphic motif was defined as Circular Shape and Mono-tone so that users can focus on the content.
credits: Creative Director by Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager by Shin Geonwoong / Design UX by Kwak Minjae Design / UI by Shin Geonwoong, Kim Seula, Park Gyeongnam / Develop by Lim Myeongseon

title: LG Sciencepark Tour Guide UI
summary: Exploring the future, one interactive experience at a time.
overview: LG Science Park is a research complex where R&D talents from various fields in LG, such as electronics, chemicals, bio, software, and telecommunications, gather in one place to create technologies for the future growth of Korea beyond LG. As LG CNS is the largest convergence research complex in Korea, it wanted to be remembered as a special experience for LG Science Park visitors by combining new services such as taking pictures with robots, introducing LG home appliances, and card news content, starting with providing a directions guiding service for visitors to use conveniently without being hung up. To create special memories with robots, The Grap worked on graphics using non-heavy colors and illustration elements.
credits: Project Manager by Lee Taikyou / Design UX by Lee Teikyou / Design UI by Seo Nayeon, Lim Hyejin, Kim Seula, Shin Geonwoong, Park Juhan / Web Publishing by Seo Nayeon

title: Qstorage Brand Website
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Illustration, Develoment
summary: Qstorage brings comfort to compact living.
overview: Qstorage is an urban self-storage service that solves the problem of accumulated luggage and small storage space right away. Qstorage is gradually expanding its branches so that you can expand your daily life and find relaxation by relieving the fatigue of small storage spaces. To strengthen the brand image, The Grap applied the vibrant orange color everywhere and used cute illustrations and soft graphic elements to create a vibrant and soft brand image. To help users to understand about the space, intuitive illustrations are used to increase usability.
credits: Project Management: Kim Hanil, Lee Taikyou / UX Design: Lee Eunsil / UI Design: Kim Hanil, Lim Hyejin / Illustration: Lim Hyejin, Gu Hyemi / Web Publishing: Jeong Soyeong

title: Cellapy Website
summary: 'Real Derma Cellapy' Set Dermatology Straight
overview: Cellapi is a brand born in dermatology and wanted to be transformed into an online cosmetic store where you can feel authenticity and professionalism, the core philosophy of the brand. To convey this, The Grap actively applied the clean image of the product. Cellapi's various product lines are placed in clear colors extracted from the product package and added intuitive icons to enjoy the simple but fun and organized product line.
credits: Project Manager_ Kim Hanil, Lee Taekyu / UX Design_ Lee Eunsil / UI Design_Kim Hanil, Lim Hyejin / Development_ Cho Cham, Lim Myeongseon

title: Promota
summary: Effortless digital marketing tools, built for small business success.
overview: Promota is an online promotional material production app that can be easily and simply produced online for small business owners who have to pay for the menu or promotion of the store everytime, and it is a service that can conveniently produce 'digital signboards', 'digital menus', and 'online flyers' through the app. Since the first launch, we are continuously considering and researching ways to provide more convenient and diverse services while updating to version 2.0. We are also providing convenience to small business owners who use our services to continue operating their templates, and we are also attempting to provide personalized services.
credits: Project Manager: Lee Taikyou, Shin Geonwoong / UI Design: Bae Youjin, Chu Sanghee / Template Design: Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin, Kim Hanil, Lee Taikyou, Bae Youjin, Kim Seula, Lim Hyejin

title: Samhwa Paint Goods
summary: Bringing color to emotion — Samhwa Paint steps into youth culture.
overview: Samhwa Paint was thinking a lot about how to get away from the existing image of a stern company and reach out to young customers. As the first step to change, The Grap participated in the production of goods and package design of a pop-up store event with "Sihyunhada Recorders." With the heart of Samhwa Paint, which wants to provide a color palette containing vivid colors of nature so that it can draw its own sensibility on the canvas of life, which cannot be measured in size, was able to attract the attention of young people with a new identity, away from the existing image of Samhwa Paint.
credits: Creative Direction by Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Management by Lee Taikyou / Design by Seula Kim, Seo Mijin

title: DOUZONE Webuilder Template
summary: From brand sites to shopping malls — modular web design made easy.
overview: The Grap participated in the construction of web builder templates of DOUZONE, a leading company that provides various business ICT solutions and services. We designed and published PC/Mobile templates to build brand sites and shopping mall sites for individuals and businesses, and modularized every elements so that anyone can create websites conveniently and easily. Modules of various designs enable the construction of desired sites for each business area through free placement.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager: Kim Hanil / UI Design: Kim Hanil

title: JTBC PLUS 4Media Websites
whatWeDid: UX Plan, UI Design, Web Publishing
summary: Four iconic magazines, one unified experience — redesigned with individuality, connected through integrated membership.
overview: The four top magazines, Elle, Cosmopolitan, Harper's Bazaar, and Esquire, which lead fashion and beauty and lifestyle trends, have been integrated and renewed by The Grap. Reflecting the client's request to operate as a common template, the design work was carried out using the individuality of each brand, and at the same time as the renewal, the establishment of an integrated membership was carried out to flexibly share the benefits with each media.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Kim Hanil / Web Publishing: Jeong Euiyoug

title: Samhwa Paint Mobile Website
summary: Where business meets beauty — Samhwa’s website reimagined for every audience.
overview: With the renewal of the existing old mobile website, we thought about how to include the corporate image of B2B, which is mainly a limited customer of industrial companies and offices, in B2C. The philosophy and values of color of Samhwa Paint have been improved in line with the current trend by transforming it from an industrial image that deals with paint to an emotional image that deals with color. By providing UX to general consumers as well as industrial/business offices so that they can easily understand color, various layers of consumers can stay longer than before on the improved website.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Kim Teawoo / UX Design: Kim Teawoo / UI Design: Shin Geonwoong, Kim Seula / Development: Jeong Euiyoung

title: Coway IoCare App
summary: Unifying devices, simplifying control — The Grap’s global UI for Coway.
overview: Starting with the design of Coway IoCare app service renewal in 2017, The Grap has been in charge of the design operation of the app until 2020. we built a UI to manage Coway's various devices as well as controls in one app, and we optimized the app so that it can respond flexibly to multiple languages according to the service globally. Also, we are building and operating Coway's B2B IoT services too.
credits: Project Manager: Seo Mijin, Kim Hanil, Shin Geonwoong / UX Design: Kim Soi, Jeong Taeyeong / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Kim Hanil, Kim Seula, Bae Youjin, Lim Hyejin, Kim Jeesung

title: Smart Living by LG & Google Cloud
summary: Bringing AI home — smart signage and seamless living experiences at LG SIGNATURE’s showroom.
overview: Elements related to the 4th industry, such as AI, IoT, and big data, have become deeply embedded in our lives. Digital signage is now being used not only in commercial spaces but also at home, which will soon become a smart home signage, providing more beneficial and convenient home environment. A service is being introduced in which the AI system checks the weather and air quality of the day by voice, checks the schedule, and handles household chores. To experience this, an experience zone was set up in the showroom of the LG SIGNATURE store in Nonhyeon-dong, and The Grap participated in the production of related digital signage, kiosks, and promotional videos.

title: Spacebank Service Platform
summary: Turning everyday spaces into shared stories — designing a human-centered platform for Spacebank.
overview: The era of sharing economy has begun where the limitations of physical space meet online platforms and are used as infinite spaces. Spacebank wanted to capture friendliness and happiness in order to establish itself as a new trend in space usage. To this end, The Grap started by putting emotional photos and stories of friendly neighbors in the main page that stimulate the curiosity about the space. With this, space has been reborn as an infinite and meaningful space by meeting the values of Spacebank. The Grap designed and provided people-space-oriented services so that the space provided by Spacebank can be more activated through online media.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Lee Taikyou / UI Design: Kim Hanil, Lee Taikyou, Kim Seonyeong

title: SK Telecom 19′ MWC Demo App
summary: A friendly take on blockchain — digital identity meets analog charm.
overview: SK Telecom, along with Deutsche Telekom, introduced a mobile identification app using blockchain at 'MWC2019'. Visitors to the exhibition hall can get a virtual ID using blockchain and use it to enter the booths of both companies and experience collecting tokens and exchanging them for prizes through content experience. Combining this journey with analog design elements (Stamp, Paper, Trip, Wallet), it became an opportunity to relieve reluctance to new services and provide a friendly experience.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager: Lee Dongyeon, Lee Taikyou / UX Design: Lee Dongyeon / UI Design: Lee Dongyeon, Lee Taikyou

title: SK Telecom Crypto Wallet
summary: Simplicity meets security — a clean, data-focused crypto wallet experience.
overview: SKT Crypto Wallet is easy to use and has strong security. You can check multiple accounts at a glance in your wallet, and you can conveniently send and receive accounts you have added to your wallet at any time. SK Telecom wanted to proceed with a function-oriented design that allows users to search for a large amount of information effectively. Because of the large amount of data and repetition, we approached it by removing unnecessary design elements as much as possible. The Grap designed it simple and plain with the visual difference of shadows according to the nature of the content and the weight contrast of typography.
credits: Creative Director: Seo Mijin, Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Lee Taikyou, Kim Seonyeong

title: COWAY 19 CES Softner GUI
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Motion Design
summary: Personalized, intuitive, and connected — Coway’s smart UI for CES blends IoT with daily wellness.
overview: There are many 'personalized services' that provides personalized content to individuals. Coway wanted the 'personalized service UI' to be applied to the products for 2019 CES exhibition, and they selected The Grap as a partner, conducted UI work on the demonstration app that is linked to the water softener and bidet and the display that will be inserted into the water softener. IoT linkage allows users to control the product and provides intuitive usability by applying colors that change smoothly depending on the condition and icons for each mode. Also, the graph and summary of the report allows users to understand the usage habits of individuals at a glance.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Kim Hanil / UI Design: Kim Hanil, Bae Youjin / 2D Motion Graphic: Park Gyeongnam

title: CLOi UI
summary: Smart service in motion — CLOi’s UX, designed for real-world interaction.
overview: In the modern world, AI has become an indispensable element. AI will continue to develop further today and in the future, closely related to all of our living environments. LG CNS has been researching these AI robots for a long time, and introduced CLOi, a robot for guiding directions and promoting. Taking advantage of our experience in robot UI, The Grap applied UX, which can provide more valuable experiences and services to customers. It is now available at Starfield and Incheon International Airport.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Lee Taikyou, Kim Hanil, Lee Seulgi

title: YSNEAKERS X SM Entertainment
summary: A fusion of fashion, fandom, and brand identity — YSNEAKERS x SM Entertainment.
overview: A chance to meet the limited edition sneaker designed by my bias! Premium custom sneakers YSNEAKERS met with SM Entertainment's Girls' Generation, Exo, to give fans a more special experience. This project has been underway since the rebranding point of SM Entertainment, and it was a time when they focused on how to express their brand identity, and the most important task was to create a site that satisfies the unique identity of each product and artist at the same time. The Grap reflected SM Entertainment's identity element prototype through interaction, suggesting the direction in which each identity is expressed. We also designed the main structure with various video contents such as the process of artist designing their own sneakers and interviews so that users can enjoy the experience.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Kim Hanil, Seo Mijin, Lee Taikyou, Kim Seonyeong, Bae Youjin / Development: Seo Daewon, Jeong Jihun

title: Running Man Animation Website + Game GUI
whatWeDid: Web UI Design, Game GUI Design
summary: Designing fun that feels right — playful, intuitive UX for the Running Man game and promotion site.
overview: The areas where UX's importance is applied in our daily lives also play a huge role in mobile games. If users feel uncomfortable with the UI when enjoying games, it will also have a big impact on playing games. The Grap participated in the Running Man animation promotion website and game GUI design for the Running Man project, which was carried out by Line Plus and SBS in partnership. It is the result of putting a lot of thought into making the game comfortable and fun for users, and trying to provide more colorful visuals by utilizing the characteristics of the game.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager: Lee Taikyou / Web UI Design: Seo Mijin Game / UI Design: Lee Taikyou, Kim Jungkwon, Kim Seonyeong, Lee Seulgi

title: YSNEAKERS Official Site
summary: Design your own — a seamless custom shoe experience reimagined by YSNEAKERS.
overview: YSNEAKERS, a luxury handmade shoe made by yourself, has renewed the site to make handmade shoes faster and more convenient for users. It consists of a floor where users can make their own designs after being provided with pre-made muses completed by expert hands so that they can explore the design that suits them before making handmade shoes. The customization stage was defined as Let's Play, providing an environment where users can make their own handmade shoes fun and quickly. It is a project that focused on fast implementation so that users do not feel stuffy during the production stage.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager: Kim Taewoo, Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Kim Taewoo / UI Design: Kang Taewoo / Development: Seo Daewon, Jeong Jihun

title: Pepper Robot UI
summary: A smarter, kinder assistant
overview: 'Pepper,' the world's first humanoid social robot capable of emotion recognition, and LGU+, have met to respond more pleasantly to customers. Born based on the AI platform developed by LG CNS and LG U+, 'Pepper' not only provides basic in-store phone/membership information, but also allows users to enjoy weather information and various games. To make it easier and more friendly to talk with 'Pepper', The Grap designed every UI/GUI in the display of 'Pepper'. Starting with Woori Bank, Kyobo Bookstore, Gachon University Gil Hospital, Lotte Department Store, and E-Mart, 'Pepper' has been faithfully serving as a friend and helper for customers in various fields so far.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Kang Taewoo, Kim Hanil, Lee Taikyou, Bae Yujin / Web Publishing: Hwang Giseop

title: Shinhan Zeroland
whatWeDid: UX Plan, UI Design, Mov Production
summary: From hesitation to humor — reimagining finance with a playful 'Zero Land' twist.
overview: The Grap made a web page and video for the promotion of the non-face-to-face commission-free event held by Shinhan Financial Investment. The non-face-to-face method, which is now familiar, was very unfamiliar at the time, and the way to convey this unfamiliarity to customers without feeling rejected was parody. By parodying the movie 'La La Land', which was very popular at the time of production, the keyword 'Zero Land' came up, and the video was also parodied and expressed cheerfully by parodying La La Land.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Management: Lim Hyeongseop, Lee Taikyou / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Kim Soohyun, Moon Seokyeong / 2D Art: Seo Mijin / Motion Graphic: Gil Youngjin

title: SK stoa
summary: Breaking the mold of TV shopping — a distinct, customer-focused UI for SK Stoa.
overview: The home shopping channels that can be seen on TV all have similar UI and design that are difficult to distinguish unless the company name is shown. We wanted to get out of the frame and thought hard about showing the independent design of SK Stoa. We made it so that the maximum visual elements can be seen in a limited TV display environment, and applied it to enable various customizations using grid-type layout. Also, we provided a design centered on 3040 women's target, which is the main customer, in order to make customers to stay more and get satisfaction among various shopping channels.
credits: Credits Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Lim Hyungseop, Lee Taikyou / UX Design: Lim Hyungseop, Jang Seungae / UI Design & Guideline: Seo Mijin, Kang Taewoo, Moon Seokyeong

title: LG CNS CMB Cloud TV
summary: Redesigning IPTV for the cloud — a cleaner, smarter TV UI for CMB’s nationwide network.
overview: In CMB Broadcasting's IPTV cloud conversion project, which has 10 cable stations across the country, The Grap participated in improving the TV UI to suit the cloud environment. Through the quick menu, we increased usability to induce to the quick menu and contents, and proceeded with the screen composition considering the scalability of additional services. Also, we expanded the range of content choices by integrating menus and changing the screen configuration so that users can check the major VOD services at a glance. Using dark color as the base, the content selection status is large, certain, and strongly expressed, and designed with clean tone to minimize complex feelings and confusion for users.
credits: Creative Director by Kim Jungkwon, Kim Suhyeon / Project Manager: Lim Hyungseop, Shin Geonwoong / UX Design: Lim Hyungseop / UI Design & Guideline: Shin Geonwoong, Lee Taegyu

title: Coway IoCare Web Service
summary: Turning data into clarity — visualizing air and water diagnostics with Coway’s IoCare web service.
overview: Coway's endless effort for clean water and air. IoCare is approaching you with a web service. How is the environment of the space where you spend your daily life? Nowadays, there is a lot of interest for the environment. Coway has been thinking a lot about how to collect data and deliver the data effectively for an accurate diagnosis of our living space. The Grap provides a special and intuitive experience with graphic elements and interactions to intuitively convey a diagnosis based on the collected data and how to compare indoor and outdoor data at a glance.
credits: Credits Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Seo Mijin / Project Manager: Seo Mijin / UXUI Design: Seo Mijin / Web Publishing: Hwang Giseop, Lim Myeongseon

title: 2025 LEE GIKWANG SOLO CONCERT [OBSESSED] IN HONGKONG
summary: Facilitating Seamless Cross-Regional Communication for the 2025 LEE GIKWANG SOLO CONCERT [OBSESSED] in Hong Kong
overview: To kick off the new year, HIGHLIGHT member Lee Gikwang brought his solo concert tour to its grand finale in Hong Kong. THE GRAP served as a vital communication bridge between the Korean and Hong Kong teams, leveraging our professional experience to support both the artist management and the event organizer. Through precise coordination and on-site assistance, we helped deliver a flawless show and created a memorable start to 2025 for both the artist and his fans in Hong Kong.
credits: Project Manager by Chang Yingjie

title: Samsung EPP Advantage Day
summary: Designed the key visual for Samsung.com's Secret Sale promotion, targeting Direct EPP members and high-value customers.
overview: Instead of highlighting discount rates, the visual focused on creating a sense of mystery and anticipation by depicting the offers as hidden behind a veil. The overall tone was carefully crafted to align with Samsung’s premium brand image. To support global localization, no text was embedded within the image. The project also included size variations and the design of a promotional eDM.
credits: Design Direction – Seo Mijin / Design – Kim Jeesung, Choi Sunhyung, Seo Mijin, Kim Dongha

title: Samsung EPP Referral Program
summary: This project defined the design concept and developed the key visuals for Samsung’s Referral Program, which offers benefits to new members invited by existing EPP (Employee Purchase Program) users.
overview: To maintain Samsung’s refined and minimal brand tone, we avoided exaggerated expressions, overly direct messaging, and off-brand color usage. Instead, we used symbolic shapes to convey meaning in a sculptural and intuitive way. We also focused on character selection to suit the target audience—students and professionals. To express a stylish and youthful mood, each figure was carefully chosen and retouched in terms of facial expression, hairstyle, and outfit.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Seo Mijin, Lim Hyejin

title: Samsung SmartThings Design Assets
summary: As part of the full redesign of the Samsung.com SmartThings webpage, we created a new set of design assets to help users intuitively understand and navigate SmartThings app services.
overview: To enhance clarity and usability, we removed unnecessary decorative elements and applied clean, information-centered visuals that clearly communicate key functions. The assets were carefully crafted to align with Samsung.com’s existing visual language while maintaining consistency across various device environments. Icons and graphic elements were designed with precision to ensure a refined and cohesive experience throughout the interface.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) Design: Seo Mijin, Choi Sunhyung, Kim Dongha

title: Monkey Travel Website
summary: Renewing the website to deliver a more adaptable and seamless user experience in a fast-changing global environment.
overview: The existing website had become outdated and was no longer aligned with modern technology or UX trends. To address this, the client aimed to enhance compatibility with rapidly evolving global APIs, deliver a consistent experience across diverse devices, improve operational efficiency, and simplify the booking process for users. The Grap carried out a full-scale UX consulting and UI, GUI design renewal for the Monkey Travel website. Built with a responsive design approach, the revamped site emphasizes user convenience and provides a streamlined, intuitive booking flow, significantly enhancing the overall user experience.
credits: Creative director by Shin Geonwoong / Design UX by Park Byung-chune / Design UI by Shin Geonwoong, Choi Sunhyung, Kim Jeesung

title: Samsung Game Portal
whatWeDid: Content Strategy, Conceptual UI Design, Proposal
summary: This project proposes a concept design for the renewal of the Samsung Game Portal, focusing on redefining the visual identity through an extended use of brand elements.
overview: Rather than relying on literal or decorative visuals, the design explores how the brand slash can evolve into a modular system of lines, triangles, grids, and icons—forming a cohesive visual language that expresses motion, energy, and clarity. The system is designed to flexibly adapt across various content formats, from immersive key visuals to UI-level details, ensuring consistency while enhancing engagement. Geometric abstraction, structural balance, and visual rhythm were prioritized to maintain a refined and future-facing brand tone.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Seo Mijin, Kim Jeesung

title: Samsung Shop App Redesign Proposal
summary: We participated in the UI redesign proposal for Samsung Shop App in collaboration with Cheil Worldwide.
overview: Based on the strategic direction defined by Cheil Worldwide, the design focused on visualizing a renewed user experience that emphasizes personalization, mobile-first usability, and seamless integration with the Samsung family of apps. While preserving key brand identity elements — including color palette, iconography, and graphic style — we applied an improved information architecture and reorganized key screens such as product recommendations and My Page. The design aims to deliver an intuitive, flexible, and consistent user interface that reflects the refined brand tone of Samsung.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Seo Mijin, Kim Hanil, Shin Geonwoong, Kim Jeesung, Lim Hyejin, Choi Sunhyung, Kim Dongha, Park Soohyun

title: ZUMP Dankook University Metaverse Campus
summary: Designing an app-free, web-based metaverse campus for Dankook University to offer easy, friendly virtual access.
overview: We designed the metaverse campus of Dankook University's Jukjeon Campus. The platform on which the metaverse was built, ZUMP, is a web-based platform with the block concept of. There is no need to install a separate app, which makes it easier for students to access meta-campus. The metaverse campus is the same as the actual appearance of Dankook University Jukjeon Campus, so that students and faculty can use it familiarly, It is designed to give users a friendly feeling by using bright and lively colors.
credits: Lead: Shin Geonwoong (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Shin Geonwoong, Kim Dongha

title: Roborock Microsite
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Motion Graphic, Web Publishing, Development
summary: Creating micro-sites to elevate the brand experience around each new product launch
overview: In line with the release of Roborock’s new products, we developed dedicated micro-sites tailored to each launch. Before the official release, we built “Coming Soon” countdown pages to build anticipation and conducted promotional events to attract early-interest customers. Each site included 3D product views, allowing users to explore the products interactively from every angle. Key features were highlighted, and both promotional videos and influencer content were available to watch. Event content on the site was regularly updated, and with every new product launch, the micro-site was refreshed and adapted to reflect the latest offering.
credits: Creative Director & Project Manager by Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong / UI Design by Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong, Lim Hyejin, Kim Jeesung, Choi Sunhyeong, Kim Dongha / Web Publishing & Development by HD Group

title: X:IN THE 3RD MINI ALBUM DESIGN
whatWeDid: Key Visual, Print Assets, Online Contents Assets, Logo
summary: Visualizing duality and self-conflict through symbolic brain imagery and apocalyptic mood.
overview: For X:IN’s 3rd mini album Defend Myself, we developed a visual concept expressing duality and inner conflict. Apocalyptic mood, mirror symmetry, synchronization, reversal, and black-and-white contrast were key themes. The brain metaphor visualizes psychological tension and self-defense, while vintage textures and sand-like fading typography express gradual erosion. We also redesigned the X:IN logo to align with the new concept and strengthen the group’s identity.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Choi Sunhyung, Kim Jeesung, Kim Dongha, Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin

title: ZUMP Website
summary: Designed ZUMP’s website with clean structure, bold visuals, and intuitive UX to enhance user engagement and clarity.
overview: ZUMP is a real-time 3D metaverse platform that enables users to create and explore virtual spaces. The Grap has created a modern, user-friendly, and creatively aligned site to suit ZUMP's vision. Designing the website with an emphasis on intuitive UX and neat visual structure with block concept which suits with the ZUMP service, it attracts continuous interest from users.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Seo Mijin, Choi Sunhyung

title: Entertainment Website Builder
whatWeDid: UX/UI Design, Web Publishing, Development, Contents Design
summary: We built a dedicated website builder platform tailored for entertainment agencies and artist labels.
overview: With a template system designed around the needs of the entertainment business, agencies can effortlessly showcase artist profiles, releases, schedules, and media content — all managed through an intuitive CMS. The platform enables entertainment brands to craft and maintain a polished online presence with ease and consistency.
credits: Creative Direction by Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin / Project Management by Shin Geonwoong / Front-end UX/UI Design by Shin Geonwoong, Choi Sunhyung / Admin UI Design by Kim Hanil / Branding & Content Assets by Seo Mijin, Kim Dongha / Web Publishing by Park Byungchune / Back-end & Admin Development by Park Byungchune

title: JoongAng Ilbo Native AD
summary: In collaboration with the Innovation Lab task force of JoongAng Ilbo, The Grap developed a series of Native Advertising web pages for Korea’s leading comprehensive daily newspaper.
overview: Starting with Amorepacific, followed by Shinsegae Duty Free and Hyundai Motor Company, we created digital storytelling experiences that blended high-quality brand content with engaging interactive design. Each campaign was built around a unique theme: “In Search of New Beauty” for Amorepacific, “The Feng Shui You Didn’t Know” for Shinsegae Duty Free, and “Racing the Crazy Road” for Hyundai Motor. Although these were promotional pages, we focused on delivering a fun and non-intrusive experience by incorporating interactive elements. The designs adopted documentary-like storytelling for the journey to find natural ingredients, a treasure-hunting travel concept for sharing local information, and an energetic, game-like feel to evoke the thrill of speed. This approach allowed each brand’s premium content to connect with audiences in an engaging and innovative way.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Kim Suhyeon, Moon Seokyeong, Bae Yujin, Kim Seonyeong / Web Publishing: Hwang Giseop

title: PANTECH iSky Brand Site
summary: With the slogan “I’m back,” the SKY IM-100 marked a bold return to the market, requiring a fresh new website to showcase its revival.
overview: Unlike previous iVEGA sites, the new iSKY website was designed to be reborn with a modern and refined identity, reflecting the premium and clean values of the SKY brand. By incorporating Pantech’s signature brand color into the design, the website seamlessly blended SKY’s distinctive simplicity and elegance throughout. The result was a website that delivered clear, intuitive communication of the IM-100’s numerous advanced features, presenting them in a way that was easy to understand without complexity.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Kim Soohyun / UX Design: Park Jeongung / UI Design: Kim Soohyun / Development: Lim Jeongtaek

title: Volkswagen Passat Launch Campaign
summary: To coincide with the launch of the 7th-generation New Passat, known for its outstanding practicality, we developed a promotional microsite highlighting the vehicle’s features and design.
overview: Based on the concept “A Picky Dad’s Choice,” the site incorporated interactive elements to present the car’s functions in a fun, engaging way, moving beyond the feel of a simple promotional page. The timeline-based storytelling structure not only delivered a narrative experience but also connected to social media integration and event participation, encouraging users to engage with the brand.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Shin Geonwoong / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop, Seo Mijin / UX Design: JS, Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong / Programing: Cho Cham

title: KT olleh tv play App
summary: In an era where people keep their smartphones closer than their TV remotes, The Grap partnered with KT, a leading IPTV service provider, to redesign the olleh tv play app.
overview: The goal was to enable viewers to search for desired content more quickly and conveniently through their phones. We streamlined the menu structure and redesigned the user interface so that viewers could easily search for VOD content and check program schedules with just one hand. Additionally, we focused on a simple and intuitive design for the My Page section, allowing users to instantly see personalized content recommendations tailored to their preferences.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Choi Yunseok, Shin Geonwoong / UX Design: Choi Yunseok / UI Design & Guideline: Shin Geonwoong, Kim Soohyun

title: HonestFund Application
summary: A user-friendly investment app for Honest Fund that simplifies product selection and highlights key data with clear, consistent design.
overview: Honest Fund’s mobile app was designed to make P2P investment simpler and more accessible. Using the brand’s signature color and red highlights to emphasize key products, the interface provides a clean, intuitive experience. Graphs and carefully organized lists help first-time investors quickly understand and select suitable products with ease.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Suhyeon / Project Manager: Lim Hyeongseop / UX Design: Lim Hyeongseop / UI Design & Guidline: Seo Mijin

title: CJ O Shopping Malaysia
summary: A streamlined, product-focused global e-commerce platform for Wowshop Malaysia, balancing CJ’s identity with clean, red-accented UI.
overview: As part of CJ O Shopping’s global expansion, The Grap participated in launching Wowshop in Malaysia, marking its third overseas project with CJ. The design focused on a simple yet impactful interface, using red accent colors to highlight key elements without conflicting with CJ’s logo or Wowshop’s own branding. Red highlights were strategically applied to draw attention to important information, while the overall UI maintained a clean and minimalist approach. Both main and product detail pages prioritized product images, with supporting specs and channel information presented in text to keep the user’s focus on the products themselves. The platform was designed for PC web and mobile web, and was also released as a hybrid app on the App Store and Google Play.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Kim Suhyeon / UI Design & Guidline: Kim Suhyeon

title: YUYU MARKET
summary: A renewed e-commerce site for YuYu Pharma, blending lifestyle products with engaging storytelling visuals and an intuitive shopping flow.
overview: As YuYu Pharma, well-known for familiar over-the-counter products like Vitamin C YooPan-C, expanded its lifestyle product lineup, The Grap supported the complete renewal and relaunch of its online market, YuYu Market. The redesigned homepage was structured to give users quick access to core products and encourage purchases, including social media-based easy sign-up options. Each product category was featured through sliding banners for a dynamic browsing experience, while muted, achromatic tones in the background helped keep the focus on the products. On product detail pages, we moved beyond simple photography, instead using storytelling-inspired images and typography to create a more engaging and memorable product presentation.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Shin Geonwoong / Project Manager: Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong / UX Design: Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong, Kim Seonae / Development: Yoon Gisu, Mun Hyeonjung

title: T-Map Taxi Motion Design
summary: A motion-enhanced redesign for Tmap Taxi, combining intuitive taxi movement graphics with smooth usability-focused interactions.
overview: Tmap Taxi, an app widely used by Korean drivers, was renewed with expanded features such as Tmap integration and Syrup Pay payment options. As part of this redesign, we developed motion graphics centered on a taxi illustration, using the intuitive visual of a taxi in motion as the base concept. To convey a sense of tension while maintaining a calm, wave-like movement that would not interfere with app usability, we carefully tested various speeds to achieve a refined animation. Additionally, for the taxi-finding feature, icon shapes on the taxi illustration were designed to change according to success or failure, making the status easy for users to recognize at a glance.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Seo Mijin / Motion Design: Lee Jongseok

title: Syrup Promotion
summary: Ongoing promotional design for Syrup, combining user-centered coupon commerce with flexible, theme-based templates.
overview: We supported SK Planet’s Syrup commerce service, which focuses on delivering the right coupons and events at the right time and place to minimize customer effort and maximize economic benefits. Our work included ongoing promotions, starting with designs tailored to highlight the unique characteristics of each event product within Syrup. To efficiently manage frequently recurring monthly promotions, we developed design templates that made it easier to operate and update event information. Even for similar promotions, we adapted the design to reflect monthly themes, providing users with fresh and engaging experiences throughout the year.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Shin Geonwoong / UI Design: Kim Suhyeon, Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin, Kim Seonae

title: DAELIM ENERGY Website
summary: A clean, globally focused website for Daelim Energy, showcasing its affordable and sustainable energy brand with clarity and precision.
overview: We developed the official global promotional website for Daelim Energy, a global supplier in the energy and infrastructure sector, to support its overseas investment and development initiatives. The site was designed to align with the company’s CI/BI guidelines, applying the designated corporate fonts and adhering to a clean, clear aesthetic. Reflecting Daelim Energy’s mission to deliver affordable, clean, and high-quality energy, the website was crafted to be as simple and straightforward as possible, ensuring clarity and ease of use for international clients.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Suhyeon / Project Manager: Shin Geonwoong / UXUI Design: Shin Geonwoong, Seo Mijin / Web Publishing: Hwang Giseop

title: Suprema BioStation T2
summary: A brand-enhancing GUI for Suprema’s BioStation T2, combining simplicity and trust with user-centered design improvements.
overview: The Grap collaborated with Suprema, a specialized biometrics technology company offering fingerprint and facial recognition solutions, to enhance the design of the BioStation T2 fingerprint recognition device. While the previous design lacked strong expression of the Suprema brand identity, the improved version incorporated Suprema’s brand colors into the clock interface — a feature users frequently view — to build a sense of trust and familiarity. The design focused on delivering a simple, reliable impression that matches Suprema’s brand image. In addition, the administrator interface was simplified to maximize usability, ensuring an intuitive and user-friendly experience.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Seo Mijin / UI Design & Guideline: Seo Mijin

title: M.NET MUSIC APP
summary: A refreshed, user-friendly redesign for M.net Music, boosting engagement through clearer visuals and improved usability.
overview: We redesigned the UI of M.net Music, the streaming service app from music entertainment channel M.net, to improve both music information delivery and overall usability. The new design elevated the visibility of album and chart information, as well as the “Music Styler” feature, encouraging greater user engagement. Transitioning from the previous dark gray theme to a white-based palette created a fresher, more inviting atmosphere. Adopting a flat design preserved simplicity, while adjusting icon sizes and enlarging touch areas minimized operational errors and enhanced user comfort.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon / UI Design: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Suhyeon, Shin Geonwoong

title: CJ O Shopping Mexico
summary: A vibrant, TV-integrated e-commerce platform for CJ O Shopping in Mexico, reflecting local culture with intuitive, icon-driven design.
overview: As part of CJ O Shopping’s global expansion, The Grap developed an e-commerce platform for the Mexican market — the second overseas project in the series. The platform was designed to highlight products currently airing on TV home shopping broadcasts, making them easy to purchase online. Drawing inspiration from CJ’s partner broadcaster Televisa, the main design incorporated an orange color palette aligned with Mexican cultural preferences. To help users intuitively understand each product category, we created hand-drawn icons and designed a densely packed layout to effectively present information in an engaging way.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Lee Migyeong / UX Design: Lee Migyeong / UI Design & Guidline: Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong

title: SUPREMA WEBSITE
summary: A brand-focused website renewal for Suprema, combining a balanced grid design with improved UX and social media integration.
overview: We redesigned the official website for Suprema, a leading company in biometric recognition and security technologies such as access control systems and fingerprint modules. The new design emphasized Suprema’s brand identity by applying a balanced, grid-based layout with a concept that makes the products appear seamlessly integrated, as if mounted on a wall. We improved the UX so that users could easily navigate to product information and conveniently download related programs through the global navigation bar. Additionally, social media integrations, including Twitter, were added to the homepage to encourage more active communication with users and build a stronger connection with the audience.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Seo Mijin / UI Design: Seo Mijin, Shin Geonwoong

title: CJ O Shopping China
summary: A fashion-magazine-inspired e-commerce platform for CJ Oshishang in China, combining fullscreen visuals with a user-friendly shopping flow.
overview: As CJ O Shopping’s first global expansion project, The Grap developed the CJ Oshishang e-commerce platform for the Chinese market. The site was designed to maximize focus on visual content, using a fullscreen layout inspired by fashion magazines to highlight product photography. To avoid a static feel, we incorporated circular graphic elements and the brand’s accent colors to create a more approachable, friendly atmosphere. Unlike typical order pages, the layout was structured to help users easily view and navigate product information, promoting a smoother and more convenient shopping experience.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Lee Migyeong / UX Design: Lee Migyeong / UI Design: Shin Geonwoong, Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun

title: LG CNS Smart Home Portal
whatWeDid: UX Design, UI Design, Guideline, Publishing
summary: A personalized, intuitive smart home portal UX for LG CNS, integrating home IoT controls with TV-based convenience and user-focused content.
overview: LG CNS showcased Korea’s first integrated home IoT solution built on a set-top box, with The Grap providing UX and UI scenarios for its core smart home portal. The portal allows users to easily control household devices and access essential home information through a TV remote or smartphone. Key features included prominently displaying the user’s name, health data, and preferred content, with content presentation differentiated by gender to deliver a more personalized experience. Pictograms and numerical data were applied to help users quickly understand the status of their home devices, while context-driven recommendations encouraged additional content and product consumption.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Project Manager: Lee Migyeong / UX Design: Lee Migyeong / UI Design: Shin Geonwoong

title: KAKAO PROMOTION
summary: Regular promotional designs for Kakao’s knowledge and community services, encouraging feature adoption through engaging event campaigns.
overview: The Grap regularly managed promotional event campaigns for Kakao’s knowledge-sharing service Tip and its community platform Cafe, operated by Korea’s largest messenger app and portal provider. These campaigns aimed to boost member engagement and naturally encourage the use of new features by motivating users to participate in events. We produced key visuals that highlighted the event’s main theme and strategically placed visual points within each page to clearly communicate the campaign’s core message and drive participation.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / UI Design: Kim Soohyun, Shin Geonwoong, Kim Seonae

title: VOLKSWAGEN NEW JETTA
summary: A lifestyle-focused microsite for Volkswagen JETTA, combining illustrations and split-screen layouts to boost user interest in test drives and events.
overview: To support the launch of Volkswagen’s compact sedan, JETTA, we developed a promotional microsite for test drive sign-ups and related events. The site approached its visuals through two concepts — imagery and illustration — to enhance appeal despite relatively limited text content. By dividing the screen into four segments and positioning the vehicle at the center, we ensured a clear focal point. Lifestyle-inspired illustrations were created to present potentially rigid information in a more flexible and engaging way, encouraging user interest within a simple microsite structure.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / UI Design: Kim Soohyun

title: SKB MOD
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun

title: Transparent Flexible Display
whatWeDid: UX Plan, GUI Design, MOV Producion
summary: A promotional concept video for Transparent Flexible Display (TFD), envisioning real-life applications and future TV UX for a next-generation experience.
overview: Alticast, a global leader in interactive digital data broadcasting solutions, collaborated with LG Display to develop Transparent Flexible Display (TFD) technology. The Grap created a promotional video illustrating how this cutting-edge display could be integrated into daily life. We developed scenarios that showed the possibilities of TFD in practical contexts, proposing a forward-looking TV UX that aligns with the display’s innovative qualities, and offering customers a compelling vision of future experiences.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / UI Design: Kim Soohyun

title: TVING WEBSITE
summary: A streamlined redesign for CJ E&M’s tving platform, enhancing entertainment engagement with intuitive navigation and a clean, grid-based content layout.
overview: CJ E&M’s official video platform tving underwent a full-scale website renewal. The core focus of this project was to boost user enjoyment within the content areas while minimizing templates to improve operational convenience. Previously cluttered menus were reorganized by restructuring the global navigation bar (GNB) and improving the information hierarchy to deliver a cleaner top-level UI and enhanced UX. A balanced grid design was applied to the wide range of programs, creating an intuitive and neat interface that allows users to explore diverse content on a single page with ease.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun

title: B1A4 TEASER PAGE
summary: A concept-driven promotional website for B1A4’s second album, guiding unofficial fans to the official platform and supporting merchandise sales.
overview: Let’s Fly, B1A4! is the official website for B1A4, a five-member boy group under WM Entertainment. This site was created to transition members of an unofficial fan-run Facebook community to the official platform and to promote the title track of their second album. The design extended the album art concept, portraying the group’s evolution from youthful boys to mature adults, while also highlighting each member’s distinct persona. Additionally, the site was structured to naturally lead to merchandise sales, maximizing engagement and fan participation.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun

title: KAKAO AD PLATFOM CONFERENCE
summary: A flexible visual identity and motion concept for Kakao’s advertising platform, evolving its language of communication through symbolic typography and dynamic graphics.
overview: akao’s No. 1 advertising service platform in Korea is constantly evolving. This evolution itself represents communication — a language expressed through actions of connecting, revealing, interacting, and acquiring, ultimately bridging advertisers and users. Building on the theme “The language and communication of Kakao’s ad platform evolves,” we designed symbolic typography reflecting the literal meanings of connect, reveal, interact, and acquire, using consonants and vowels as a visual language. Considering scalability, the typography was developed across a full range of vowels, English letters, and variable weights. Kakao’s unique color palette was complemented with accent edge colors, symbolizing the cross-color dynamic of evolution. The accompanying video concept, titled “Intergalactic Communication Protocol,” visualized rotating circles of various shapes to illustrate Kakao’s capacity to carry diverse content.
credits: Creative Director: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Design: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / Motion Graphic: Lee Jongseok

title: LEE EUN GYEOL PROJECT WEBSITE
summary: An immersive, scroll-and-keyboard-navigated website for Eungyeol Project, highlighting the artistry and dynamic performances of Korea’s leading magic production company.
overview: We developed the official website for Eungyeol Project, a premier magic performance production company leading the Korean magic industry. The design placed powerful live performance images front and center to convey a sense of mystery and wonder. Users can navigate vertically and horizontally using mouse scroll or keyboard arrow keys, exploring pages that feature magician Eungyeol’s performance videos, profile, and company information. To reflect the vivid, exciting nature of a live performance agency, the overall experience was designed to feel energetic and engaging.
credits: Project Manager: Kim Jungkwon, Kim Soohyun / UI Design: Kim Soohyun

title: Cetaphil
summary: A storytelling-driven promotional video for Cetaphil, highlighting Galderma’s skincare philosophy through relatable narration and friendly character visuals.
overview: We produced a promotional video for Cetaphil, the flagship brand of Galderma, a Swiss global pharmaceutical company driven by the philosophy “a vibrant life starts with excellent skincare.” The video narrates the journey of Cetaphil — from its hospital-based introduction and growing word-of-mouth reputation to Galderma’s ongoing commitment — while spotlighting its three key features: cleansing, moisturizing, and protecting. To make the story more relatable and directly appealing to consumers, we used friendly character illustrations in a white-toned mood, complemented by simple, metaphorical shapes based on Cetaphil’s brand colors to convey a sense of warmth and approachability.
credits: Motion Graphic: Lee Jongseok

title: LG Pra.L App
industry: IoT, App, Beauty Tech, UX/UI, Motion Graphic
summary: A Smart Beauty Solution for LG Pra.L that seamlessly connects high-tech devices with hyper-personalized digital experiences.
overview: In alignment with LG Pra.L’s brand renewal, we planned and designed a Smart Beauty Solution that seamlessly connects with high-tech beauty devices. By embodying the brand philosophy of "Beauty advanced by tech," we established a sophisticated and trustworthy visual system. Through data-driven hyper-personalized UX, the platform suggests customized routines and content optimized for individual skin concerns. Notably, we maximized usability by streamlining complex device pairing and control processes into an intuitive controller interface, realizing a seamless connection between hardware and digital environments.
credits: Project Manager: Jang Seungae / UX Design: Kim Hanil, Kim Jeesung, Choi Sunhyung

title: X:IN THE 4TH MINI ALBUM DESIGN
whatWeDid: Key Visual, Print Assets, Online Contents Assets, Logo
summary: Designing an album visualizing X:IN’s new futuristic identity with new aesthetics
overview: The album HYPER is a project that visually unravels X:IN’s own futurism based on the narrative of them scouting the Earth from the moon. It is composed of thermal imaging graphic elements and layered with surveillance camera motifs and noise/blur effects to create a sense like a "screen under observation." By deploying interface elements in which information is scanned and recorded, the perspective in the world view itself has been built as a design system beyond simple conceptual expression. The album name and track logo are refined to function as a device that complements the alien mood within the entire graphic system, consistently connecting the world view and visuals.
credits: Lead: Seo Mijin (Project Management & Design Direction) / Design: Kim Dongha, An Jinsung, Seo Mijin
